title: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney who resigned amid ethics scandal, reportedly running again for Suffolk DA
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rachael Rollins, former U.S. Attorney, is running again for Suffolk County District Attorney after filing nomination papers.
Rollins resigned in May 2023 as U.S. Attorney amid ethics concerns after a Justice Department investigation.
Current DA Kevin Hayden and attorney Linda Champion are also running in the Suffolk County DA election.

title: Firefighters ask for support for colleague critically injured in training academy fall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Chelmsford firefighter Nick Spinale, 28, critically injured after falling 40 feet during training at Massachusetts Firefighting Academy.
Spinale sustained severe injuries and requires multiple surgeries, currently in intensive care at UMass Memorial Medical Center.
Chelmsford Firefighters Local 1839 union seeks community support for Spinale's recovery and acknowledges widespread support received.

title: How an old Roman Anthony TikTok video inspired Red Sox’ new celebration
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox have introduced a new doubles celebration inspired by a TikTok video featuring Roman Anthony.
Manager Alex Cora revealed the celebration's origins from a TikTok dance to Mariah Carey's 'Obsessed.'
Outfielder Roman Anthony has a .217 average with three extra-base hits in his first 12 games of the 2026 season.

title: Former Mass. police chief charged with committing fraud while leading department
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former Adams police chief, Kevin Scott Kelley, 46, charged with fraud for false overtime claims totaling nearly $20,000.
Kelley allegedly submitted fraudulent overtime claims from January 2024 to September 2025 under a municipal traffic enforcement grant.
Adams police and State Police investigation led to grand jury indictment; arraignment scheduled for May 4 in Berkshire Superior Court.

title: North Andover police unions call for ‘immediate removal’ of chief after Fitzsimmons acquittal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: North Andover police unions voted no confidence in Chief Charles Gray, citing leadership failures and lack of body cameras.
More than 90% of union members call for Gray's removal, blaming him for 'toxic culture' and poor accountability.
The case of former officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons highlighted issues with department policies, leading to union's dissatisfaction.

title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics are reportedly considering a trade for Giannis Antetokounmpo from the Milwaukee Bucks.
Giannis Antetokounmpo is under contract until 2028, but the Bucks' struggles might lead to exploring trade options.
Celtics face financial challenges, with Antetokounmpo's potential salary pushing them deep into the luxury tax.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox win 5-0 against Brewers, earning their first series win of the season.
Sonny Gray pitches 6 1/3 scoreless innings, lowering his ERA to 2.76.
Tyler Samaniego strikes out the side in his MLB debut, joining Fred Wenz in Red Sox history.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu proposed a $4.9 billion budget for fiscal year 2027, with a 2.1% increase in spending.
Factors affecting the budget include a $48.4 million deficit, police overtime costs, and rising health care expenses.
Residential property taxes increased 13% this year; Wu called for repealing state restrictions on municipal tax levies.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame, chosen by a 20-person committee.
Gronkowski, a first-time finalist, is a five-time Pro Bowler, leading in total touchdowns and second in receiving yards for the Patriots.
Fan voting will determine the inductee, with results announced in the week following the April 28 vote conclusion.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed top prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract for the 2025-26 season.
Hagens, 19, played six games for Providence, scoring one power-play goal and three assists before joining the NHL roster.
Hagens could bolster Boston's lineup, particularly on the power play, ahead of their expected playoff run.
